subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear section headings and numbered subsections guide the reader through a logical progression.
Each part begins with an orientation sentence and ends with a link to the next topic, reinforcing coherence.
Use of tables and a concluding section enhances the structural clarity.
weaknesses: No separate abstract and some overlapping headings (two âEvaluationâ sections) limit exceptional organisation.
Redundant background anecdotes and repeated paragraphs reduce conciseness.
Some tables lack clear labels, requiring the reader to infer which columns correspond to which team.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and well organized, with clear structure and logical development (band 3). However, recurring redundancies and missing abstract or distinct labels prevent it from reaching the top band, and some content could be more concise (band 2 for conciseness). A score of 3 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Consistent and correct presentation of the Poisson formula and notation in block form.
Effective use of tables and a scatter plot to represent data and trends.
Logical sequence of formula introduction, data substitution, and interpretation supports clear communication.
weaknesses: Variable formatting is inconsistent (italic vs upright) in prose.
Graph axes lack labels and units, hindering full clarity.
Some representations (e.g., duplicated probability table) could be enhanced (heatâmap or conditional formatting).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used mostly consistently and accurately, with only minor formatting lapses (band 3). Multiple representations (tables, formulas, plot) are employed effectively but not exemplarily (band 3). The logical structure is good, though occasional repetition slightly weakens clarity (band 3). Overall, this criterion merits a score of 3.

strengths: The personal rationale linked to the studentâs own interest in Hammarby IF is clear and consistent.
Data collection and bespoke attack/defence ratio computations show significant independent thinking.
Title page and framing immediately orient the reader to the studentâs personal engagement.
weaknesses: The modelling approach follows standard Poisson and regression templates without innovative extensions.
No exploration of alternative methods (e.g. Monte Carlo simulation) limits creativity.
Personal narrative, while present, mirrors common analytics articles rather than introducing a novel perspective.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is clear evidence of independent thinking and a personal approach, and the student takes initiative in data collection (band 2). However, the work lacks exceptional creativity or highly innovative personal insights (band 2). A score of 2 reflects strong but not outstanding personal engagement.

strengths: The conclusion clearly evaluates model strengths and weaknesses and suggests concrete improvements (xG, cost-per-point).
Reflection sections quantify model accuracy and acknowledge simplifying assumptions.
Discussion of RÂ², comparison of Poisson vs regression, and identification of omitted factors demonstrate thoughtful analysis.
weaknesses: Reflection remains surface-level and occasionally repetitive, without deeper sensitivity analyses.
Future implications are general and not quantified, limiting the depth of evaluation.
The conclusion rehashes earlier limitations rather than offering new insights or directions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of results and process (band 2), with clear evaluation of outcomes (band 2). However, the reflection lacks the depth, critical insight, and comprehensive future implications required for the highest band. A score of 2 is therefore appropriate.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (Poisson modelling and regression) is applied directly to actual data and clearly interpreted.
Mathematical techniques lie squarely at SL level and are applied correctly to address the research question.
Probability calculations align with Î» values, demonstrating solid understanding and accuracy.
weaknesses: Key formula displays are missing or blank (e.g. defence strength formula).
Variable labelling errors and inconsistent rounding appear in tables.
Numerical values for regression parameters (a and b) are omitted, reducing completeness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is appropriate, relevant, and applied correctly at SL level (band 4 relevance, band 3 level, band 3 understanding). Minor errors and omissions prevent a higher band. Overall, a score of 3 reflects good use of mathematics with room for more comprehensive detail.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within each mathematical section the student introduces the formula â substitutes data â interprets the result, which gives a clear logical chain.  Occasional breaks (e.g. defence strength description copied twice) slightly weaken clarity, so the strand fits the âgood logical structureâ band.

Formula derivations are accurate and probabilities in the table align with Î» values.  Small inconsistencies (defence ratio mis-labelled, probability matrix rounded inconsistently, no calculation evidence for regression parameters) show understanding is good but not flawless.  Thus âgood understanding with minor errorsâ fits.

The student employs formulas, multi-row tables, and a scatter-plot with trend-line.  These representations aid understanding and are generally appropriate, though the graph lacks full axis labelling units and one probability matrix is duplicated with percentages rather than shown as a heat-map.  Effectiveness is good but not exemplary.

The report is divided into clear sections (Introduction, Exploration, Evaluation, Conclusion) and the narrative follows a logical progression from motivation â data collection â model construction â results â critique.  Flow is rarely disrupted and the reader can follow how each mathematical step feeds the next.  Minor lapses (e.g., repeated background about Hammarbyâs 2022/23 positions) prevent the work from reaching the âexcellentâ standard but overall coherence is solidly in the âcoherent and logically developedâ band.

The student shows initiative by constructing a probability matrix and comparing predicted vs. actual results, but creative extensions (e.g. alternative distributions, Monte-Carlo simulation) are absent.  Hence, creativity is âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ.

The student analyses RÂ², contrasts Poisson with regression, and identifies factors (injuries, tactics) that the model omits.  This is thoughtful but not deeply quantitative; no sensitivity tests or error estimates are provided.  Therefore the strand aligns with âthoughtful analysisâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ.

The conclusion evaluates model strengths/weaknesses and suggests improvements (xG, machine learning, cost-per-point).  These are clear and connected to findings, satisfying âclear evaluationâ, yet future implications remain general and unquantified, so the highest band is not justified.

Reflection appears in Sections 2.5, 3 and 5 where the student quantifies model accuracy (80 % outcome, 20 % exact score) and acknowledges simplifying assumptions.  These comments show meaningful reflection, but discussion remains surface-level and sometimes repetitive, so it does not reach âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Correct symbols (Î», e, P(X = x)) are used consistently and equations are typeset clearly.  Minor slipsâattack-strength ratio labelled A for defence, un-italicised variables inside proseâoccur but do not impede comprehension, hence âmostly consistentâ rather than âfully consistentâ usage.

The exploration is clearly framed around the studentâs own interest in Hammarby IF and articulates personal rationale throughout.  Still, much of the narrative mirrors common football-analytics articles and does not introduce an especially innovative perspective, placing it comfortably in the second band.

Most content is relevant to the research question, yet the introduction contains several anecdotal paragraphs that do not advance the mathematics, and some explanations (e.g. definitions of points system, basic Poisson parameters) could be condensed.  These redundancies keep the exploration from being fully concise.

Both Poisson modelling and non-linear regression are directly pertinent to the research aims and carried through to interpretation.  The mathematics is applied to actual match data and spending figures, demonstrating effective relevance.  Scope is adequate for SL but not exhaustive enough for âcomprehensive (5)â or âexpert (6)â levels.

Choosing a personal club, collecting bespoke data and manually computing attack/defence ratios show significant independent thinking beyond textbook examples.  However, the modelling approach (standard Poisson method and simple regression) follows well-known templates rather than novel methods, so the evidence is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

Headings, numbered subsections, tables and a concluding section demonstrate good structural planning.  Each part begins with a short orientation sentence and ends with a link to the following topic.  However, there is no separate abstract, and some headings (e.g. two different âEvaluationâ sections) overlap, so the work is not âexceptionallyâ organised.

Poisson distribution, least-squares linearisation, and RÂ² calculation lie squarely at SL standard and are applied correctly.  No HL-level techniques (e.g. matrix algebra, calculus-based optimisation) are attempted, so the work is âcommensurate with course levelâ (3), not âfullyâ or âexpertlyâ beyond SL.

annotations.comment: The section on mathematical models provides a solid overview of Poisson distribution and trend analysis. To deepen engagement, the student could briefly justify why alternative distributions were not selected.
Some sentences in the modelâdescription section restate earlier points about forecasting goals. Streamline this paragraph to avoid unnecessary repetition and keep content relevant.

The introduction contains extensive anecdotal detail that does not directly advance the mathematical exploration. Consider condensing background about fan experiences to focus more succinctly on the research question.
The Aim and Rationale clearly states the research question and personal motivation for studying Hammarby IFâs spending-performance link. This frames the exploration with strong coherence.
Section headings are clear and help the reader follow the explorationâs logical flow. The use of numbered subsections enhances overall organization.
The opening paragraph of the Exploration restates the introduction almost verbatim. Consider summarizing the context and focusing quickly on the new analytical content.
The Poisson distribution formula is presented correctly and typeset clearly in block form, demonstrating accurate mathematical notation.
The list describing attack and defence strengths is clear, but notation varies (sometimes italic, sometimes upright). Ensure consistent formatting of variables throughout prose.

The defence strength formula is referenced but the actual equation display is blank. Include the explicit formula $$DS = \frac{\text{goals conceded}}{\text{league average conceded}}$$ for clarity and completeness.
In the defence calculation for MalmÃ¶ FF, variable labels repeat Hammarby IF. Correct the team name to avoid confusion and ensure accurate interpretation.
The comparison of predicted vs actual outcomes is thorough and supports critical reflection on model accuracy. Highlight which matches most clearly illustrate model strengths.
The model accuracy section succinctly quantifies outcome vs scoreline predictions. To deepen analysis, include a brief sensitivity test on parameter variation (e.g. Î» Â±10%).
Table 1 effectively presents probabilities up to 5 goals for each team. To enhance clarity, include a brief note on rounding conventions and the significance of decimals.
The raw probability matrix is informative but static. Consider replacing the second raw table with a heat-map or conditional formatting to highlight high-probability outcomes effectively.
Table 3âs percentages presentation lacks clear column labels for Hammarby and MalmÃ¶. Explicitly label each axis in the caption to avoid reader confusion over which row corresponds to which team.
Listing the Allsvenskan points system is clear, but this standard information could be moved to a brief footnote or appendix to maintain conciseness in the main body.
The data table of spending vs points is wellâstructured and supports subsequent regression. For deeper insight, annotate any clear outliers like BK HÃ¤cken.
The description of data organization is verbose. Tighten language and focus on key variables to improve conciseness and maintain relevance.
The powerâlaw regression equation is introduced but numerical values for parameters a and b are not provided. Include these to demonstrate full understanding and accuracy.
The scatter plot is effective in illustrating the spending vs points relationship, but axis labels and units are missing. Adding clear labels will greatly improve communication.
The evaluation of model strengths provides concrete examples linking spending to performance, demonstrating thoughtful analysis of results.
The section on possible improvements outlines meaningful extensions (xG, cost-per-point) and indicates reflective thinking about limitations and next steps.
The conclusion effectively recaps both models and their accuracy but rehashes earlier limitations without offering new insights. A more concise summary with future direction would strengthen closure.
The title page clearly states topic and methods, immediately orienting the reader to both context and analytical approach.
